text: Maps are among the oldest tools humans have used to make sense of the world, translating the vast and complex geography of the earth into something we can hold in our hands and study at a glance. They are simultaneously practical objects and works of art, reflecting not just physical terrain but the priorities, assumptions, and even biases of whoever made them. A map of the same territory drawn in different centuries can reveal as much about the mapmakers as it does about the land itself.
